description: Has every binding identifiable from the binding, its referenced source, and the document's discovery context alone, with no dependency on external registries, vendor catalogs, or environment configuration.
notes: Binding sufficiency is a semantic property of how a tool resolves a ref against its source artifact. Negative cases are difficult to express as inline JSON because 'requires external information to resolve' is a property of the ref's interpretation, not its surface form. The positive cases below show clearly self-sufficient bindings; structural negative cases (e.g., refs that conventionally require an environment-resolved registry lookup) require harness-level interpretation of ref schemes and are not represented in this corpus.

tests.description: binding with self-sufficient ref into embedded source content
binding with self-sufficient JSON Pointer ref into externally located source
binding with absent ref (targets the source as a whole per the format's convention)
